introSection: RipTide is a travelling steel roller coaster built by Italian manufacturer SDC currently owned by US carnival operator Deggeller Attractions.

sections.title: History
sections.content: The ride was previously owned by EK Fernandez Shows from 2004 or earlier. It was painted red and was called Wild Cat. At some point from 2009 to 2011, it was purchased by Deggeller Attractions who painted it blue and renamed it RipTide.

sections.title: Incident
sections.content: On July 3, 2011 a train stalled, reportedly due to a rider's hat falling off and becoming stuck in the wheels. A second train crashed into the back of the stalled train. Six people were taken to hospital as a result. Three lawsuits were launched by riders against Deggeller Attractions, who claimed the guest was not allowed to take the hat on board.

fullText:
RipTide is a travelling steel roller coaster built by Italian manufacturer SDC currently owned by US carnival operator Deggeller Attractions.

The ride was previously owned by EK Fernandez Shows from 2004 or earlier. It was painted red and was called Wild Cat. At some point from 2009 to 2011, it was purchased by Deggeller Attractions who painted it blue and renamed it RipTide.





On July 3, 2011 a train stalled, reportedly due to a rider's hat falling off and becoming stuck in the wheels. A second train crashed into the back of the stalled train. Six people were taken to hospital as a result. Three lawsuits were launched by riders against Deggeller Attractions, who claimed the guest was not allowed to take the hat on board.
